title: Microsoft Entra ID Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Microsoft Entra ID** (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft's cloud identity and access management directory. This connector — `EntraIdConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "entra_id"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) — wraps the operational identity surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Entra tenant, all reached via the **Microsoft Graph v1.0 API** under `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0`:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users` | List, get, create, update, delete |
| Groups | `/groups` | List + read groups |
| Group Members | `/groups/{id}/members` + `/$ref` | List, add, remove |
| Applications | `/applications` | List app registrations |
| Service Principals | `/servicePrincipals` | List service principals |
| Directory Roles | `/directoryRoles` | List activated roles |
| Role Assignments | `/roleManagement/directory/roleAssignments` | List RBAC assignments |
| Sign-In Logs | `/auditLogs/signIns` | List sign-in events |
| Audit Logs | `/auditLogs/directoryAudits` | List directory audit events |
| Conditional Access | `/identity/conditionalAccess/policies` | List CA policies |
| Devices | `/devices` | List registered devices |
| Domains | `/domains` | List verified domains |

The connector returns raw Microsoft Graph JSON to callers (camelCase wire format), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), pages `/users` via the standard `@odata.nextLink` cursor pattern, and normalises **users** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`) during `sync()`. The `tenant_id` baked into the doc id is the **Shielva** tenant — distinct from the `azure_tenant_id` config (the Entra/AAD tenant GUID that scopes the OAuth token).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Register an app in Azure Portal

1. Sign in to https://portal.azure.com.
2. Navigate to **Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations → New registration**.
3. Name the app (e.g. "Shielva Entra ID Connector"), pick **single tenant** or **multi-tenant** (choose single for most cases), leave Redirect URI blank (this is a daemon app).
4. Click **Register**. From the **Overview** blade copy:
   - **Application (client) ID** → `client_id`
   - **Directory (tenant) ID** → `azure_tenant_id`

### 2. Create a client secret

5. Open **Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret**.
6. Pick an expiry (24 months max), click **Add**.
7. Copy the **Value** column immediately — Azure shows it only once → `client_secret`.

### 3. Grant Microsoft Graph application permissions

8. Open **API permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Application permissions**.
9. Add at least the permissions your workload needs:
   - `User.Read.All` — list/get users + health check
   - `User.ReadWrite.All` — create/update/delete users
   - `Group.Read.All` — list groups, group members
   - `Group.ReadWrite.All` — create groups, add/remove members
   - `Directory.Read.All` — applications, service principals, directory roles, role assignments, devices, domains
   - `AuditLog.Read.All` — sign-in + directory audit logs
   - `Policy.Read.All` — conditional access policies
10. Click **Grant admin consent for {tenant}** — a global admin must consent for application permissions to take effect.

### 4. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Microsoft Entra ID**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `azure_tenant_id` (text) | Directory (tenant) ID GUID from step 1 |
| `client_id` (text) | Application (client) ID GUID from step 1 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | Client secret VALUE from step 2 |
| `scopes` (text, optional) | `https://graph.microsoft.com/.default` (default) |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0` (default; override for sovereign clouds) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 240 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the three required fields (it does NOT call Microsoft Graph), initialises the `EntraIdHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`.

### 5. Authorize + health check

`authorize()` POSTs the client-credentials grant to `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{azure_tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token` and caches the minted bearer.

`health_check()` lists 1 user via `GET /users?$top=1&$select=id` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Microsoft Entra ID uses the **OAuth2 client-credentials grant** (app-only / daemon flow) (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`). No user interaction, no consent screen at install time, no refresh tokens.

### Credential model

- `azure_tenant_id` — Entra/AAD tenant GUID (or `common` / `organizations`). Used in the token URL path segment. Distinct from the Shielva tenant id.
- `client_id` — Application (client) ID from your Azure App registration.
- `client_secret` — Client secret value.
- `scopes` — Always `https://graph.microsoft.com/.default` for app-only flows. The literal `.default` tells Microsoft identity to issue a token with **every application permission the admin has consented to** — there is no fine-grained per-call scope.

### Token endpoint

```
POST https://login.microsoftonline.com/{azure_tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
&client_id={client_id}
&client_secret={client_secret}
&scope=https://graph.microsoft.com/.default
```

Response:
```json
{
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 3599,
  "ext_expires_in": 3599,
  "access_token": "eyJ0eXAi…"
}
```

**Note:** client-credentials returns no `refresh_token`. The connector re-mints by re-issuing the same POST when the cached token is within 60 seconds of expiry.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `azure_tenant_id` + `client_id` + `client_secret`. Initialises `EntraIdHTTPClient`. Does **not** call Microsoft Graph. |
| `authorize(auth_code='', state='')` | Runs the client-credentials grant. Returns a `TokenInfo` (with `refresh_token=None`). The `auth_code` / `state` parameters are accepted for surface compatibility with `oauth2_code` connectors and are ignored. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users?$top=1&$select=id` — minimal probe. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | Re-mints via `authorize()`. |
| token storage | Cached in-memory inside `EntraIdHTTPClient`; also written to BaseConnector's `_token_info` via `set_token()` after `authorize()`. |

### Graph header contract

Every outbound request to `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/*`:

```
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}
Accept: application/json
Content-Type: application/json   ← when posting a body
```

### Required Microsoft Graph application permissions

| Capability | Permission |
|---|---|
| List / read users (`list_users`, `get_user`, `health_check`, `sync`) | `User.Read.All` |
| Create / update / delete users | `User.ReadWrite.All` |
| List / read groups, group members | `Group.Read.All` |
| Create groups, add / remove members | `Group.ReadWrite.All` |
| Applications, service principals, directory roles, role assignments, devices, domains | `Directory.Read.All` |
| Audit logs + sign-in logs | `AuditLog.Read.All` |
| Conditional Access policies | `Policy.Read.All` |

Grant the **minimum** set your tenant actually uses — Microsoft Graph permissions are extremely powerful at the directory level. Admin consent is required for all application permissions.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `azure_tenant_id` | text | yes | `self.azure_tenant_id` | Token URL path segment. Distinct from Shielva tenant_id. |
| `client_id` | text | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth client id |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth client secret |
| `scopes` | text | no | `self.scopes` (defaults to `https://graph.microsoft.com/.default`) | OAuth scope |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0`) | Override only for sovereign clouds (US Gov, China, Germany). |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 240) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["azure_tenant_id", "client_id", "client_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_GRAPH_BASE = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0"
_DEFAULT_SCOPE = "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"
```

### Sovereign cloud endpoints

| Cloud | Login base | Graph base |
|---|---|---|
| Global | `https://login.microsoftonline.com` | `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0` |
| US Gov GCC High | `https://login.microsoftonline.us` | `https://graph.microsoft.us/v1.0` |
| China (21Vianet) | `https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn` | `https://microsoftgraph.chinacloudapi.cn/v1.0` |
| Germany | `https://login.microsoftonline.de` | `https://graph.microsoft.de/v1.0` |

For sovereign clouds: override `base_url` in install_fields AND pass a matching `token_url` when constructing the HTTP client (the connector exposes `EntraIdHTTPClient(... , token_url=...)` for that).

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `EntraIdConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::EntraIdHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `azure_tenant_id` + `client_id` + `client_secret`; initialise `EntraIdHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API. Returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)` on success, `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` on failure.

### `async authorize(auth_code='', state='') -> TokenInfo`
Runs the OAuth2 client-credentials grant against `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{azure_tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token`. Returns a `TokenInfo` with `refresh_token=None` (client_credentials has no refresh). Cached inside the HTTP client; persisted to BaseConnector via `set_token()`.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> TokenInfo`
BaseConnector hook — re-mints via `authorize()`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 user via `GET /users?$top=1&$select=id` as a lightweight probe (avoids requiring `Directory.Read.All` that `/organization` would need). Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages `/users?$top=100&$select=...` and follows `@odata.nextLink` until exhausted. Each user is normalised into a `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{shielva_tenant_id}_{user_id}"`) and ingested. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async list_users(top=100, filter=None, search=None, orderby=None, select=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users` with `$top`, `$filter`, `$search` (auto-quoted), `$orderby`, `$select` (joined with commas).

### `async get_user(user_id_or_upn, select=None) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{id|userPrincipalName}`. Raises `EntraIdNotFound` on 404.

### `async create_user(account_enabled, display_name, mail_nickname, password, user_principal_name, force_change_password_next_signin=True) -> Dict`
`POST /users`. Body shape:
```json
{
  "accountEnabled": true,
  "displayName": "...",
  "mailNickname": "...",
  "userPrincipalName": "user@domain.com",
  "passwordProfile": {
    "forceChangePasswordNextSignIn": true,
    "password": "..."
  }
}
```

### `async update_user(user_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /users/{id}` with `fields` as the body verbatim. Raises `ValueError` on empty fields.

### `async delete_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /users/{id}`.

children.title: Groups
children.content:
### `async list_groups(top=100, filter=None, select=None) -> Dict`
`GET /groups`.

### `async get_group(group_id) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{id}`.

### `async create_group(display_name, mail_nickname, mail_enabled=False, security_enabled=True, description=None, group_types=None) -> Dict`
`POST /groups`.

### `async list_group_members(group_id, top=100) -> Dict`
`GET /groups/{id}/members`.

### `async add_group_member(group_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`POST /groups/{id}/members/$ref` with body `{"@odata.id": "{graph_base}/directoryObjects/{user_id}"}`.

### `async remove_group_member(group_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /groups/{gid}/members/{uid}/$ref`.

children.title: Applications & Service Principals
children.content:
### `async list_applications(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /applications` — app registrations in the tenant.

### `async list_service_principals(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /servicePrincipals` — service principals (the per-tenant identity for an app).

children.title: Directory Roles & Role Assignments
children.content:
### `async list_directory_roles() -> Dict`
`GET /directoryRoles` — activated directory roles (Global Reader, User Administrator, etc.).

### `async list_role_assignments(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /roleManagement/directory/roleAssignments` — who has which role.

children.title: Audit & Sign-In Logs
children.content:
### `async list_signin_logs(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /auditLogs/signIns` — sign-in events (user, app, status, conditional access result).

### `async list_audit_logs(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /auditLogs/directoryAudits` — directory change events (role assignments, group membership changes, app consent, etc.).

children.title: Devices · Conditional Access · Domains
children.content:
### `async list_devices(top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /devices` — registered/joined devices.

### `async list_conditional_access_policies() -> Dict`
`GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` — CA policy definitions.

### `async list_domains() -> Dict`
`GET /domains` — verified tenant domains.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
EntraIdError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── EntraIdAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── EntraIdBadRequestError            # 400
├── EntraIdNotFound                   # 404
├── EntraIdConflictError              # 409
├── EntraIdRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
├── EntraIdServerError                # 5xx
└── EntraIdNetworkError               # transport / DNS / TLS
```

Back-compat aliases preserved (`EntraIDError`, `EntraIDAuthError`, `EntraIDNotFound`, `EntraIDNetworkError`).

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately (typed exception) |
| 401 | Refresh token once, retry the request. If it 401s again → `EntraIdAuthError`. |
| 429 | Exponential backoff honouring `Retry-After` header. Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`. |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `_RETRY_DELAY_S * _BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt` capped at 32s. Retry up to 3. |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `EntraIdNetworkError`. |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `azure_tenant_id`, `client_id`, or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter all three in ACP. The client secret VALUE is shown only once at creation — generate a new one if lost. |
| `AADSTS7000215: Invalid client secret provided` at authorize | Wrong client_secret (often the secret ID was copied instead of the VALUE) | In Azure → App registrations → Certificates & secrets, generate a new secret and copy the **Value** column (not the secret ID). |
| `AADSTS700016: Application not found in the directory` | `client_id` belongs to a different Entra tenant than `azure_tenant_id` | Verify both GUIDs come from the SAME tenant's app registration. |
| `AADSTS50034: The user account ... does not exist in the {tenant} directory` | Using a user-OAuth client_id for a daemon flow | Client_credentials requires an App registration, NOT a user. Check that App registration → Authentication → Allow public client flows = NO. |
| `AADSTS500011: The resource principal named ... was not found` | Microsoft Graph application permission missing OR admin consent not granted | In Azure → App registrations → API permissions, add the Microsoft Graph application permission AND click **Grant admin consent for {tenant}**. |
| 403 `Authorization_RequestDenied: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation` | App registration lacks the specific Graph permission for the call | Add the permission (e.g. `Group.ReadWrite.All` for `add_group_member`) AND re-grant admin consent. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — token cache stale or admin revoked permissions | Connector auto-refreshes once; if it still 401s, re-grant admin consent + rotate the client secret. |
| `entra_id.http.retry status=429` warnings spike | Hitting per-app Graph throttle | Connector retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_user("alice@contoso.com")` | UPN typo OR the user is a B2B guest with `mail` ≠ UPN | Try `userPrincipalName` exactly as it appears in `list_users`. For guests this is usually `alice_external.com#EXT#@contoso.onmicrosoft.com`. |
| `Sync returns 0 users` | App lacks `User.Read.All` OR tenant has 0 enabled users | Grant `User.Read.All` + admin consent. Verify with `list_users(top=1)`. |
| Sovereign cloud calls fail with 404 / DNS error | Using global URLs in a sovereign tenant | Override `base_url` to `https://graph.microsoft.us/v1.0` (US Gov) / `.de` / `.cn` AND construct the HTTP client with the matching `token_url` (e.g. `https://login.microsoftonline.us/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token`). |
| `EntraIdConflictError` (409) on `create_user` | `userPrincipalName` already taken (Entra UPNs are tenant-unique) OR `mailNickname` already taken in the directory | Pick a unique UPN/mailNickname. UPN format: `<localpart>@<verified-domain>`. |
| Sign-in / audit log queries return empty `value` | Logs require Entra ID **P1 or P2** license tier | Verify license in Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → Overview → License. |
